content.text:
Here are the results for your query:

## "The Geometry of Truth" — Marks & Tegmark at COLM 2024

**The Paper:**

The paper "The Geometry of Truth: Emergent Linear Structure in LLM Representations of True/False Datasets" by **Samuel Marks** (Northeastern University) and **Max Tegmark** (MIT) was published as a conference paper at COLM 2024.

The paper studies how Large Language Models represent truth and falsehood internally. It notes that while recent work has trained probes on LLM internal activations to infer truthfulness, these approaches have been controversial due to generalization failures. The authors use high-quality true/false datasets and find clear linear structure in LLM representations.

Their evidence comes from three lines:

1.
Visualizations of LLM true/false statement representations, which reveal clear linear structure.
Transfer experiments in which probes trained on one dataset generalize to different datasets.
Causal evidence obtained by surgically intervening in a LLM's forward pass, causing it to treat false statements as true and vice versa.
COLM took place at the Zellerbach Theater in the Annenberg Center for the Performing Arts at the University of Pennsylvania between October 7–9, 2024.
The conference was a three-day single-track event with a mix of invited talks, oral paper presentations, and poster sessions.
The paper is available on arXiv ([2310.06824](https://arxiv.org/abs/2310.06824)) and on [OpenReview](https://openreview.net/forum?id=aajyHYjjsk).
